content:
[Image: This image is a company logo. It consists of a graphic icon positioned above a text element, set against a plain white background. **Graphic Icon:**
The icon is a red square containing white, concentric, curved lines. These lines emanate from the bottom-left corner and sweep upward and toward the right, resembling radio waves, signal patterns, or ripples. In the bottom-right corner of the square, there is a solid red shape defined by a curve that follows the movement of the white lines. **Text Element:**
Directly below the icon, the word "**ACCELIO**" is written in large, black, uppercase letters. The typeface is a sophisticated font with varying stroke widths, characterized by clean lines and a modern feel. A small "**TM**" (trademark symbol) is located at the bottom right of the text. **Overall Purpose:**
The image serves as the visual identity for the brand "Accelio." The combination of the wave-like graphic and the brand name is designed to represent the company's professional identity.]

content:
This sample consists of a simple form containing four distinct fields. The data file contains eight
separate records. By default, the data file will produce a PDF file containing eight separate pages. The selective
use of the bookmark file will produce the same PDF with a separate pane containing
bookmarks. This screenshot of the sample output shows a PDF file with bookmarks. [Image: This image is a screenshot of an older version of the **Acrobat Reader** software displaying a PDF document titled "**ap_bookmark.pdf**". The interface is divided into a standard application layout including a menu bar, a toolbar, a navigation sidebar, and a main document viewing pane. **Interface Components:**
* **Menu Bar:** Located at the top, containing the options: File, Edit, Document, View, Window, and Help. * **Toolbar:** A row of icons for various functions, such as file management, zooming, the hand tool, search, text selection, and page navigation (arrows and page icons). * **Navigation Sidebar:** On the left, the "**Bookmarks**" tab is selected. It shows a hierarchical list under a heading titled "**Invoices by Date**." This list contains several entries, each with a document icon, dated from "**2000-01-1**" to "**2000-01-8**." The entry "**2000-01-1**" is highlighted in bold, indicating it is the currently selected bookmark. * **Main Viewing Pane:** The large area on the right displays the content of the selected bookmark. It shows a rectangular box containing monospaced text that provides details for the specific invoice:
    * **Date:** 2000-01-1
    * **Description:** Description for item # 1
    * **Type:** TYPE1
    * **Amount:** 11.00

**Relationships and Purpose:**
The image illustrates the relationship between a PDF's **bookmarks** and its **content**. By selecting a specific date in the sidebar hierarchy, the user can instantly navigate to the corresponding page in the document. In this instance, selecting the first bookmark ("2000-01-1") has triggered the main pane to display the specific data associated with that invoice date. The overall purpose of the image is to demonstrate the bookmarking and navigation functionality within the Acrobat Reader software.]
The left pane displays the available bookmarks for this PDF.

figures.description:
This image is a screenshot of an older version of the **Acrobat Reader** software displaying a PDF document titled "**ap_bookmark.pdf**". The interface is divided into a standard application layout including a menu bar, a toolbar, a navigation sidebar, and a main document viewing pane.

**Interface Components:**
* **Menu Bar:** Located at the top, containing the options: File, Edit, Document, View, Window, and Help.
* **Toolbar:** A row of icons for various functions, such as file management, zooming, the hand tool, search, text selection, and page navigation (arrows and page icons).
* **Navigation Sidebar:** On the left, the "**Bookmarks**" tab is selected. It shows a hierarchical list under a heading titled "**Invoices by Date**." This list contains several entries, each with a document icon, dated from "**2000-01-1**" to "**2000-01-8**." The entry "**2000-01-1**" is highlighted in bold, indicating it is the currently selected bookmark.
* **Main Viewing Pane:** The large area on the right displays the content of the selected bookmark. It shows a rectangular box containing monospaced text that provides details for the specific invoice:
    * **Date:** 2000-01-1
    * **Description:** Description for item # 1
    * **Type:** TYPE1
    * **Amount:** 11.00

**Relationships and Purpose:**
The image illustrates the relationship between a PDF's **bookmarks** and its **content**. By selecting a specific date in the sidebar hierarchy, the user can instantly navigate to the corresponding page in the document. In this instance, selecting the first bookmark ("2000-01-1") has triggered the main pane to display the specific data associated with that invoice date. The overall purpose of the image is to demonstrate the bookmarking and navigation functionality within the Acrobat Reader software.

This image is a company logo. It consists of a graphic icon positioned above a text element, set against a plain white background.

**Graphic Icon:**
The icon is a red square containing white, concentric, curved lines. These lines emanate from the bottom-left corner and sweep upward and toward the right, resembling radio waves, signal patterns, or ripples. In the bottom-right corner of the square, there is a solid red shape defined by a curve that follows the movement of the white lines.

**Text Element:**
Directly below the icon, the word "**ACCELIO**" is written in large, black, uppercase letters. The typeface is a sophisticated font with varying stroke widths, characterized by clean lines and a modern feel. A small "**TM**" (trademark symbol) is located at the bottom right of the text.

**Overall Purpose:**
The image serves as the visual identity for the brand "Accelio." The combination of the wave-like graphic and the brand name is designed to represent the company's professional identity.

The left pane displays the available bookmarks for this PDF. You may need to enable the
display of bookmarks in Adobe  Acrobat  Reader by clicking Window > Show Bookmarks . Selecting a date from the left pane displays the corresponding page within the document. Note that the index has been sorted according to the specification in the bookmark file, and that
pages within the file are created according to the original order in the data file. [Image: This image is a company logo. It consists of a graphic icon positioned above a text element, set against a plain white background. **Graphic Icon:**

figures.description:
This image is a screenshot of an older version of the **Acrobat Reader** software displaying a PDF document titled "**ap_bookmark.pdf**". The interface is divided into a standard application layout including a menu bar, a toolbar, a navigation sidebar, and a main document viewing pane.

**Interface Components:**
* **Menu Bar:** Located at the top, containing the options: File, Edit, Document, View, Window, and Help.
* **Toolbar:** A row of icons for various functions, such as file management, zooming, the hand tool, search, text selection, and page navigation (arrows and page icons).
* **Navigation Sidebar:** On the left, the "**Bookmarks**" tab is selected. It shows a hierarchical list under a heading titled "**Invoices by Date**." This list contains several entries, each with a document icon, dated from "**2000-01-1**" to "**2000-01-8**." The entry "**2000-01-1**" is highlighted in bold, indicating it is the currently selected bookmark.
* **Main Viewing Pane:** The large area on the right displays the content of the selected bookmark. It shows a rectangular box containing monospaced text that provides details for the specific invoice:
    * **Date:** 2000-01-1
    * **Description:** Description for item # 1
    * **Type:** TYPE1
    * **Amount:** 11.00

**Relationships and Purpose:**
The image illustrates the relationship between a PDF's **bookmarks** and its **content**. By selecting a specific date in the sidebar hierarchy, the user can instantly navigate to the corresponding page in the document. In this instance, selecting the first bookmark ("2000-01-1") has triggered the main pane to display the specific data associated with that invoice date. The overall purpose of the image is to demonstrate the bookmarking and navigation functionality within the Acrobat Reader software.

This image is a company logo. It consists of a graphic icon positioned above a text element, set against a plain white background.

**Graphic Icon:**
The icon is a red square containing white, concentric, curved lines. These lines emanate from the bottom-left corner and sweep upward and toward the right, resembling radio waves, signal patterns, or ripples. In the bottom-right corner of the square, there is a solid red shape defined by a curve that follows the movement of the white lines.

**Text Element:**
Directly below the icon, the word "**ACCELIO**" is written in large, black, uppercase letters. The typeface is a sophisticated font with varying stroke widths, characterized by clean lines and a modern feel. A small "**TM**" (trademark symbol) is located at the bottom right of the text.

**Overall Purpose:**
The image serves as the visual identity for the brand "Accelio." The combination of the wave-like graphic and the brand name is designed to represent the company's professional identity.

The typeface is a sophisticated font with varying stroke widths, characterized by clean lines and a modern feel. A small "**TM**" (trademark symbol) is located at the bottom right of the text. **Overall Purpose:**
The image serves as the visual identity for the brand "Accelio." The combination of the wave-like graphic and the brand name is designed to represent the company's professional identity.]
Sample Data File
Sample Bookmark File
^reformat trunc
[invoices]
^symbolset WINLATIN1
Invoices by Date=0
^field trans_date
trans_date=1,A
2000-01-1
[type]
^field description
Invoices by Item Type=0
Description for item #1
trans_type=1,A
^field trans_type
[amount]
TYPE1
Invoices by Transaction Amount=0
^field trans_amount
trans_amount=1,D
11.00
^page 1
^field trans_date
2000-01-2
^field description
Description for item #2
^field trans_type
TYPE2
^field trans_amount
11.00
^page 1
^field trans_date
2000-01-3
^field description
Description for item #3
^field trans_type
TYPE3
The example bookmark file includes three distinct sections:
•
Invoices sorted, ascending, by date. •
Invoices sorted, ascending, by item type. •
Invoices sorted, descending, by transaction amount.

figures.description:
This image is a screenshot of an older version of the **Acrobat Reader** software displaying a PDF document titled "**ap_bookmark.pdf**". The interface is divided into a standard application layout including a menu bar, a toolbar, a navigation sidebar, and a main document viewing pane.

**Interface Components:**
* **Menu Bar:** Located at the top, containing the options: File, Edit, Document, View, Window, and Help.
* **Toolbar:** A row of icons for various functions, such as file management, zooming, the hand tool, search, text selection, and page navigation (arrows and page icons).
* **Navigation Sidebar:** On the left, the "**Bookmarks**" tab is selected. It shows a hierarchical list under a heading titled "**Invoices by Date**." This list contains several entries, each with a document icon, dated from "**2000-01-1**" to "**2000-01-8**." The entry "**2000-01-1**" is highlighted in bold, indicating it is the currently selected bookmark.
* **Main Viewing Pane:** The large area on the right displays the content of the selected bookmark. It shows a rectangular box containing monospaced text that provides details for the specific invoice:
    * **Date:** 2000-01-1
    * **Description:** Description for item # 1
    * **Type:** TYPE1
    * **Amount:** 11.00

**Relationships and Purpose:**
The image illustrates the relationship between a PDF's **bookmarks** and its **content**. By selecting a specific date in the sidebar hierarchy, the user can instantly navigate to the corresponding page in the document. In this instance, selecting the first bookmark ("2000-01-1") has triggered the main pane to display the specific data associated with that invoice date. The overall purpose of the image is to demonstrate the bookmarking and navigation functionality within the Acrobat Reader software.

Invoices sorted, ascending, by date.
•
Invoices sorted, ascending, by item type.
•
Invoices sorted, descending, by transaction amount.
embedding_text:
Document: c4611_sample_explain.pdf
Section Path: Cover > Overview
Pages: 1–3

This sample consists of a simple form containing four distinct fields. The data file contains eight
separate records.
By default, the data file will produce a PDF file containing eight separate pages. The selective
use of the bookmark file will produce the same PDF with a separate pane containing
bookmarks. This screenshot of the sample output shows a PDF file with bookmarks.
[Image: This image is a screenshot of an older version of the **Acrobat Reader** software displaying a PDF document titled "**ap_bookmark.pdf**". The interface is divided into a standard application layout including a menu bar, a toolbar, a navigation sidebar, and a main document viewing pane.

**Interface Components:**
* **Menu Bar:** Located at the top, containing the options: File, Edit, Document, View, Window, and Help.
* **Toolbar:** A row of icons for various functions, such as file management, zooming, the hand tool, search, text selection, and page navigation (arrows and page icons).
* **Navigation Sidebar:** On the left, the "**Bookmarks**" tab is selected. It shows a hierarchical list under a heading titled "**Invoices by Date**." This list contains several entries, each with a document icon, dated from "**2000-01-1**" to "**2000-01-8**." The entry "**2000-01-1**" is highlighted in bold, indicating it is the currently selected bookmark.
* **Main Viewing Pane:** The large area on the right displays the content of the selected bookmark. It shows a rectangular box containing monospaced text that provides details for the specific invoice:
    * **Date:** 2000-01-1
    * **Description:** Description for item # 1
    * **Type:** TYPE1
    * **Amount:** 11.00

**Relationships and Purpose:**
The image illustrates the relationship between a PDF's **bookmarks** and its **content**. By selecting a specific date in the sidebar hierarchy, the user can instantly navigate to the corresponding page in the document. In this instance, selecting the first bookmark ("2000-01-1") has triggered the main pane to display the specific data associated with that invoice date. The overall purpose of the image is to demonstrate the bookmarking and navigation functionality within the Acrobat Reader software.]
The left pane displays the available bookmarks for this PDF. You may need to enable the
display of bookmarks in Adobe  Acrobat  Reader by clicking Window > Show Bookmarks .
Selecting a date from the left pane displays the corresponding page within the document.
Note that the index has been sorted according to the specification in the bookmark file, and that
pages within the file are created according to the original order in the data file.
[Image: This image is a company logo. It consists of a graphic icon positioned above a text element, set against a plain white background.

**Graphic Icon:**
The icon is a red square containing white, concentric, curved lines. These lines emanate from the bottom-left corner and sweep upward and toward the right, resembling radio waves, signal patterns, or ripples. In the bottom-right corner of the square, there is a solid red shape defined by a curve that follows the movement of the white lines.

**Text Element:**
Directly below the icon, the word "**ACCELIO**" is written in large, black, uppercase letters. The typeface is a sophisticated font with varying stroke widths, characterized by clean lines and a modern feel. A small "**TM**" (trademark symbol) is located at the bottom right of the text.

**Overall Purpose:**
The image serves as the visual identity for the brand "Accelio." The combination of the wave-like graphic and the brand name is designed to represent the company's professional identity.]
Sample Data File
Sample Bookmark File
^reformat trunc
[invoices]
^symbolset WINLATIN1
Invoices by Date=0
^field trans_date
trans_date=1,A
2000-01-1
[type]
^field description
Invoices by Item Type=0
Description for item #1
trans_type=1,A
^field trans_type
[amount]
TYPE1
Invoices by Transaction Amount=0
^field trans_amount
trans_amount=1,D
11.00
^page 1
^field trans_date
2000-01-2
^field description
Description for item #2
^field trans_type
TYPE2
^field trans_amount
11.00
^page 1
^field trans_date
2000-01-3
^field description
Description for item #3
^field trans_type
TYPE3
The example bookmark file includes three distinct sections:
•
Invoices sorted, ascending, by date.
•
Invoices sorted, ascending, by item type.
•
Invoices sorted, descending, by transaction amount.

figures.description:
This image is a screenshot of an older version of the **Acrobat Reader** software displaying a PDF document titled "**ap_bookmark.pdf**". The interface is divided into a standard application layout including a menu bar, a toolbar, a navigation sidebar, and a main document viewing pane.

**Interface Components:**
* **Menu Bar:** Located at the top, containing the options: File, Edit, Document, View, Window, and Help.
* **Toolbar:** A row of icons for various functions, such as file management, zooming, the hand tool, search, text selection, and page navigation (arrows and page icons).
* **Navigation Sidebar:** On the left, the "**Bookmarks**" tab is selected. It shows a hierarchical list under a heading titled "**Invoices by Date**." This list contains several entries, each with a document icon, dated from "**2000-01-1**" to "**2000-01-8**." The entry "**2000-01-1**" is highlighted in bold, indicating it is the currently selected bookmark.
* **Main Viewing Pane:** The large area on the right displays the content of the selected bookmark. It shows a rectangular box containing monospaced text that provides details for the specific invoice:
    * **Date:** 2000-01-1
    * **Description:** Description for item # 1
    * **Type:** TYPE1
    * **Amount:** 11.00

**Relationships and Purpose:**
The image illustrates the relationship between a PDF's **bookmarks** and its **content**. By selecting a specific date in the sidebar hierarchy, the user can instantly navigate to the corresponding page in the document. In this instance, selecting the first bookmark ("2000-01-1") has triggered the main pane to display the specific data associated with that invoice date. The overall purpose of the image is to demonstrate the bookmarking and navigation functionality within the Acrobat Reader software.

This image is a company logo. It consists of a graphic icon positioned above a text element, set against a plain white background.

**Graphic Icon:**
The icon is a red square containing white, concentric, curved lines. These lines emanate from the bottom-left corner and sweep upward and toward the right, resembling radio waves, signal patterns, or ripples. In the bottom-right corner of the square, there is a solid red shape defined by a curve that follows the movement of the white lines.

**Text Element:**
Directly below the icon, the word "**ACCELIO**" is written in large, black, uppercase letters. The typeface is a sophisticated font with varying stroke widths, characterized by clean lines and a modern feel. A small "**TM**" (trademark symbol) is located at the bottom right of the text.

**Overall Purpose:**
The image serves as the visual identity for the brand "Accelio." The combination of the wave-like graphic and the brand name is designed to represent the company's professional identity.

content:
To deploy this sample in your environment:
1. Open the template design ap_bookmark.IFD in Output Designer and recompile the
template for the appropriate presentment target. 2. Modify the -z option in the ^job command in the data file ap_bookmark.dat to:
•
Identify the target output device. •
Identify the bookmark file using the -abmk command. •
Identify the section for which to generate bookmarks, if desired, using the -abms
command. For example,
| To bookmark by … | Use the command line parameter … |
|---|---|
| To bookmark by … | Use the command line parameter … |
| Invoices | -abmkap bookmark.bmk -abmsinvoices
_ |
| Type | -abmkap bookmark.bmk -abmstype
_ |
| Amount | -abmkap bookmark.bmk -abmsamount
_ |
To bookmark by … Use the command line parameter …
-abmk ap_bookmark.bmk -abms invoices
Invoices
-abmk ap_bookmark.bmk -abms type
Type
-abmk ap_bookmark.bmk -abms amount
Amount
[Image: This image is a company logo. It consists of a graphic icon positioned above a text element, set against a plain white background. **Graphic Icon:**
The icon is a red square containing white, concentric, curved lines. These lines emanate from the bottom-left corner and sweep upward and toward the right, resembling radio waves, signal patterns, or ripples. In the bottom-right corner of the square, there is a solid red shape defined by a curve that follows the movement of the white lines. **Text Element:**
Directly below the icon, the word "**ACCELIO**" is written in large, black, uppercase letters. The typeface is a sophisticated font with varying stroke widths, characterized by clean lines and a modern feel. A small "**TM**" (trademark symbol) is located at the bottom right of the text. **Overall Purpose:**
The image serves as the visual identity for the brand "Accelio." The combination of the wave-like graphic and the brand name is designed to represent the company's professional identity.]
3. Place the accompanying files in directories consistent with your implementation:
•
Place ap_bookmark.IFD in the Designs subdirectory for Output Designer.
